pattern: Unbounded coin selection is an optimal-substructure problem: the fewest coins for an amount is one more than the best over each coin subtracted, filled bottom-up for every amount up to the target.
hints: The best way to make an amount reuses the best way to make a smaller amount, since each coin can be used any number of times.
Build up answers for every value from zero to the target, where making zero needs no coins.

For each amount a, try every coin c that is no larger than a and take one plus the best answer for a minus c; leave amounts that stay unreachable marked as impossible and report minus one if the target stays impossible.

edge_inputs.description: amount unreachable with the only coin
greedy by largest coin would be suboptimal here
huge coin denominations far exceed the amount
no coin divides into the odd amount

stress.description: random small denominations and a moderate amount
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randrange(1, 13)
    coins = sorted(set(rng.randrange(1, 50) for _ in range(k)))
    if not coins:
        coins = [1]
    amount = rng.randrange(0, min(size, 10001))
    return [coins, amount]
stress.note: small coins keep many amounts reachable so the DP exercises real transitions
